13	# Feature: Federation / Hub-Spoke Coordinator
14	
15	**ID:** FEAT-026
16	**Status:** Frame
17	**Priority:** P2
18	**Owner:** DDx Team
19	
20	## Overview
21	
22	Federation lets multiple `ddx server` nodes present a unified view of beads,
23	runs, workers, and projects across machines while each node remains fully usable
24	on its own. One node runs as the **hub**; the others run as **spokes** that
25	register and heartbeat to the hub. The hub aggregates reads by fanning out to
26	spokes' existing GraphQL APIs over ts-net. Basic operator writes are
27	owner-targeted: worker start/stop, bead queue mutations, and spec/document
28	writes may be forwarded to the node that owns the selected project, but writes
29	are never broadcast.
30	
31	This feature covers **read-federation spokes**: nodes with inbound DDx server
32	surfaces that the hub can query. FEAT-029 / ADR-028 cover **managed nodes**:
33	machines that do not expose a ts-net listener and instead dial the hub over an
34	outbound control channel for full remote control.
35	
36	ADR-007 captures the topology, authority model, ts-net policy, plain-HTTP
37	opt-out, and write-routing contract. ADR-006 supplies the transport-layer
38	authentication. FEAT-020 supplies node identity, the project registry, and
39	the persisted state model that federation-state.json extends. FEAT-021
40	supplies the `/nodes/:nodeId/...` URL grammar that the hub resolves across
41	registered spokes.
42	
43	## Problem Statement
44	
45	**Current situation:** Every `ddx server` is a single node. The UI sees only
46	the projects on its own machine; CLI tools cannot ask one server about work
47	happening on another. Operators with more than one machine — workstation +
48	server, or several developer boxes on a tailnet — must visit each UI
49	separately and stitch the picture together by hand.
50	
51	**Pain points:**
52	- No single dashboard answers "what is happening across my machines?"
53	- Combined views (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`, `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`) are
54	  pre-wired in FEAT-021 for cross-project but stop at the node boundary.
55	- Run history, bead queues, and project lists must be reconciled by hand
56	  when work spans nodes.
57	- Story 15 (server-side prompt execution) needs a routing contract for
58	  "which node owns this row" before it can forward writes safely.
59	
60	**Desired outcome:** An operator can run `ddx server --hub-mode` on one node
61	and `ddx server --hub=<host>` on the others. The hub UI surfaces a
62	`/federation` overview, all `/nodes/:nodeId/...` routes resolve across the
63	federation, combined views accept `?scope=federation` to fan out across spokes,
64	and the hub can start/stop workers plus mutate beads/specs on the owning node.
65	Spoke UIs continue to work directly as a fallback.
66	
67	## Architecture
68	
69	See **ADR-007** for the topology, authority, ts-net policy, write-routing
70	contract, naming convention, and version-handshake compatibility matrix.
71	This spec describes the user-visible surface that ADR-007 makes real.
72	
73	### Roles
74	
75	| Role | Flag | Behavior |
76	|------|------|----------|
77	| `standalone` | (default) | Single node; no `/api/federation/*`; no register/heartbeat. |
78	| `hub` | `--hub-mode` | Exposes `/api/federation/*`; persists registry; fans out reads. |
79	| `spoke` | `--hub=<host>` | Registers and heartbeats to `<host>`; serves federated reads via existing `/graphql`. |
80	| `hub_spoke` | `--hub-mode --hub=<other>` | Both behaviors on the same node. |
81	
82	`/api/node` exposes the active role as `node.federation_role`
83	(see FEAT-020 amendment).
84	
85	### Federation Registry
86	
87	Persisted on the hub at `~/.local/share/ddx/federation-state.json`:
88	
89	```json
90	{
91	  "schema_version": "1",
92	  "hub": {
93	    "node_id": "node-7029e8d6",
94	    "node_name": "eitri",
95	    "started_at": "2026-05-02T19:58:33Z"
96	  },
97	  "spokes": [
98	    {
99	      "node_id": "node-bf91c204",
100	      "node_name": "bragi",
101	      "url": "https://bragi:7743",
102	      "ddx_version": "0.42.0",
103	      "schema_version": "1",
104	      "graphql_schema_version": "2026-04-29",
105	      "capabilities": ["beads", "runs", "projects", "doc-graph"],
106	      "registered_at": "2026-05-02T19:58:34Z",
107	      "last_heartbeat": "2026-05-02T20:00:01Z",
108	      "compat_status": "live",
109	      "liveness": "live"
110	    }
111	  ]
112	}
113	```
114	
115	`compat_status` is one of `live`, `degraded`, `rejected`. `liveness` is one
116	of `live`, `stale`, `offline` (see ADR-007).
117	
118	### Hub HTTP API
119	
120	| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
121	|----------|--------|---------|
122	| `/api/federation/register` | POST | Idempotent register on stable `node_id`; returns assigned compat_status |
123	| `/api/federation/heartbeat` | POST | 30s + jitter; updates `last_heartbeat` and re-asserts URL |
124	| `/api/federation/deregister` | POST | Graceful shutdown; removes spoke from registry |
125	| `/api/federation/nodes` | GET | List of `{hub, spokes[]}` with liveness + compat_status |
126	
127	All federation endpoints enforce ts-net or loopback peers unless the hub was
128	started with `--federation-allow-plain-http` (see ADR-007).
129	
130	### Federated GraphQL Read Surface
131	
132	The hub exposes parallel federated fields alongside existing local queries.
133	Existing queries remain node-local and unchanged; federated fields fan out:
134	
135	```graphql
136	type Query {
137	  federationNodes: [FederationNode!]!
138	  federatedBeads(scope: FederationScope, projectID: ID): BeadConnection!
139	  federatedRuns(scope: FederationScope, projectID: ID, layer: RunLayer): RunConnection!
140	  federatedProjects(scope: FederationScope): [Project!]!
141	}
142	
143	enum FederationScope { LOCAL FEDERATION }
144	```
145	
146	Every federated row carries the routing-metadata fields described in
147	ADR-007 (`node_id`, `project_id`, `project_url`, `project_path`,
148	`write_capability`, `status`).
149	
150	### Owner-Targeted Write Surface
151	
152	Federation write routing is a narrow operator-control path, not data
153	replication. The hub may forward only commands that target one owning node and
154	one owning project. It must never broadcast a write to multiple spokes.
155	
156	Forwardable basic-operator commands:
157	
158	| Command | Owning target | Purpose |
159	|---|---|---|
160	| `startWorker` / `workerDispatch(kind: "work")` | project owner | Start one or more autonomous `ddx work` workers on that node. |
161	| `stopWorker` | worker owner | Request stop for a running worker on the node that owns the worker id. |
162	| `beadCreate`, `beadUpdate`, `beadApprove`, `beadBlock`, `beadCancel`, `beadReopen` | project owner | Modify the owning project's bead queue. |
163	| `documentWrite` | project owner | Save project-confined spec/document changes, especially `docs/helix/**`. |
164	
165	Forwarded writes carry:
166	
167	- origin identity: the operator identity seen by the hub
168	- forwarding path: hub node id plus target spoke node id
169	- request id / idempotency key
170	- target node id and project id
171	- target revision or expected version when the mutation needs stale-write
172	  protection
173	
174	The owning node is authoritative. It may reject a forwarded write because the
175	project is missing, the node is offline/stale, the requested write capability is
176	absent, path confinement fails, the worker cap would be exceeded, the bead state
177	changed, or the idempotency key maps to a previous result.
178	
179	### CLI Flags
180	
181	```
182	--hub-mode                       Run as federation hub
183	--hub=<host[:port]>              Run as spoke; register with this hub
184	--federation-allow-plain-http    Allow non-ts-net peers (hub-mode required to accept;
185	                                 spokes need it too to send)
186	```
187	
188	Precedence (matches ADR-006): flag > env > config (`.ddx.yml` config is a
189	follow-up).
190	
191	### UI Routes (delivered by FEAT-021 amendment)
192	
193	- `/federation` — federation overview (hub + spokes, liveness, compat).
194	- `/nodes/:nodeId/...` — when the node is a registered spoke, the hub
195	  resolves and serves the route from the spoke's data.
196	- Combined views (`/nodes/:nodeId/beads`, `/nodes/:nodeId/runs`) accept
197	  `?scope=federation` to fan out across all spokes.
198	
199	## Requirements
200

ID:       ddx-3c399bec
Title:    server: add stale-safe documentWrite version checks and graph refresh
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:iterate, area:server, area:federation, area:docs, kind:feature, plan:server-operator-workbench
Parent:   ddx-aa88732d
Desc:     PROBLEM
Spec saves from the GraphQL API can still overwrite a document when the caller's optimistic concurrency token is stale, and the current mutation schema exposes only path and content. The resolver does rebuild the graph after writes, but there is no explicit stale-write gate or conflict response before the filesystem is mutated.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 defines DocumentWrite with only path and content and writes directly with os.WriteFile.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without an expected hash/version input.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:62-89 rebuilds the graph after the write, but only after the filesystem has already been changed.

PROPOSED FIX

Desc:     PROBLEM
Hub-mode already has a generic owner-targeted mutation transport, but documentWrite still follows the local filesystem write path. A spoke-owned project edited from the hub would therefore persist on the hub filesystem instead of the owning spoke.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_docs.go:34-55 writes document content locally after path resolution.
- cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go:81-198 already implements ForwardMutation with origin identity, forwarding path, target node/project, expected version, and typed refusal handling.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go:7-9 documents that the federation provider surface already includes owner-targeted write forwarding.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3313-3319 exposes documentWrite(path, content) without a routing-aware input.

PROPOSED FIX
- Detect hub-mode spoke-owned document targets in the resolver and route them through Federation.ForwardMutation instead of local os.WriteFile.
- Carry origin identity, forwarding path, request id, idempotency key, target node/project IDs, and expected version through the forward request and returned response.
- Preserve local path confinement for same-node writes and for non-hub requests.
- Surface typed refusal errors for offline, stale, read-only, missing-owner, or broadcast-like targets instead of silently falling back to local state.

NON-SCOPE
- Stale-content conflict checking and graph rebuild behavior are owned by sibling bead ddx-3c399bec.
- Playwright spec-editing coverage stays with ddx-e951255c.

ID:       ddx-3f3e1cbe
Title:    federation: prove live owner-targeted writes route to spoke
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, area:federation, area:server, area:web, kind:test, story:14, spec:FEAT-026
Parent:   ddx-a038a090
Deps:     ddx-b303cf96
Desc:     PROBLEM
The federation suite proves hub/spoke registration, federated reads, offline badges, restart recovery, and mocked UI mutation states, but it does not yet prove the most important live owner-targeted write contract: commands issued from a hub for a spoke-owned project must execute on the spoke and must not create hub-local phantom state.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:34-39 says FEAT-026/ADR-007 require owner-targeted federation writes and that hub/spoke writes must never broadcast.
- docs/helix/06-iterate/server-operator-workbench-plan-2026-06-08.md:98-102 requires forwarding for `startWorker`, `stopWorker`, bead mutations, and `documentWrite`, with the hub never creating local fallback state for a spoke write.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:335-337 requires spoke worker start/stop forwarding and offline refusal.
- docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md:350 and :363 require federated bead and document writes.
- cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts:219-307 currently exercises live two-node reads/offline/restart but not live owner-targeted write persistence.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the live multi-process federation coverage, preferably in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` or a sibling e2e file, to start one hub and one spoke with isolated temp roots and per-node XDG state.
- From the hub, issue a worker start/stop for a spoke project and assert the worker exists in the spoke project worker registry, not the hub registry.
- From the hub, create or edit a bead in the spoke project and assert the spoke `.ddx/beads.jsonl` changes while the hub project does not receive a phantom local bead.
- From the hub, write a `docs/helix/**` document in the spoke project and assert the file changes on the spoke root only and origin/forwarding metadata is observable where the API exposes it.
- Include an offline-spoke refusal assertion for at least one write path.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add real ts-net/MagicDNS coverage; loopback hub/spoke is the autonomous local gate.
- Do not add screenshot assertions.
- Do not implement managed-node outbound control from ADR-028; this bead covers FEAT-026 inbound spoke owner writes only.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-a038a090. Depends on the stale-bead reconciliation bead so the implementation target is narrowed before coding.
Accept:   1. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_WorkerStartStopRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued start/stop for a spoke project creates/stops exactly one spoke-local worker and no hub-local phantom worker.
2. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_BeadMutationRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves a hub-issued bead create/edit for a spoke project mutates the spoke project and not the hub project.
3. `TestFederationLiveOwnerWrites_DocumentWriteRoutesToSpoke` or a Playwright test with equivalent title/steps proves hub-issued `documentWrite` for a spoke `docs/helix/**` path writes only under the spoke root and preserves/returns forwarding metadata where available.
4. At least one live write path includes an offline-spoke refusal assertion with no phantom hub-local row.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/... ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... -run 'Federation|FanOut|RouteMutation|ForwardMutation|Spoke' -count=1` passes.\n6. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bunx playwright test e2e/federation-2node.spec.ts` passes, or the new sibling e2e file named in this bead passes.\n7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

## Summary

Investigated bead `ddx-359e812b`. AC1–3 (tracker narrowing, test names, reconciliation citations) were already satisfied by the committed bead body — no changes needed there. AC4's Go test half passes, but the Playwright half (`beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts`, `spec-editing.spec.ts`) fails 8/10 tests, and not narrowly — it's broken across unrelated local-only flows too (create bead, edit bead, lifecycle actions, spec save, stale-write refusal).

Root-caused via git history: commit `cc1ab1954` ("Merge origin/main into main", 2026-07-02) silently discarded ~3,868 lines of already-implemented, already-tested federation write-forwarding code (from closed beads ddx-e81c01de/ddx-1644ead2/ddx-abd08e19/ddx-3c399bec/ddx-c3b15c32) plus matching frontend UI (~2,000+ lines in `BeadDetail.svelte`, document editing pages). Recovering that is a multi-bead-sized job that this bead's own NON-SCOPE line explicitly forbids ("do not reintroduce the mocked Playwright smoke paths"), and it's already owned by sibling bead `ddx-3f3e1cbe` (priority 1, open) which targets a live two-node e2e proof instead.

Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` (status: blocked) documenting this with full evidence and recommending AC4 be corrected or the bead closed as superseded by `ddx-3f3e1cbe`. No commit made — nothing in this bead's scope needed code changes, and reverting one stray build artifact (`build/.gitkeep`, touched by the Playwright webServer's build step) restored the tree to clean.
